stories.headline: New Zealand actor Sam Neill, known for 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' dies at 78, his family says - AP News
stories.summary: Sam Neill has died at age 78. He was an elegant, versatile actor who moved from art films to blockbusters like “Jurassic Park” and “The Hunt for Red October.” He said he had a rare type of lymphoma i…
stories.why: Neill was the thinking man's action hero who proved you could do art films and blockbusters without compromising—his death marks the end of that era of actor versatility in mainstream cinema.

stories.headline: Horoscope for Monday, 07/13/26 by Christopher Renstrom - SFGATE
stories.summary: TAURUS (April 19 - May 19): Never underestimate the power of telling it like it is. State facts as you see them and you'll win the support you seek.
stories.why: This is filler content padding entertainment sections; skip it entirely.

stories.headline: Greece awaits Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey’ with anticipation despite casting controversy - AP News
stories.summary: Christopher Nolan’s film adaptation of Homer’s “The Odyssey” opens July 17 amid international anticipation and controversy over its casting. In Greece, where the 3,000-year-old epic is taught in ever…
stories.why: Nolan betting his prestige on adapting an ancient text signals Hollywood's shift toward "elevated" IP—but the casting backlash reveals the audience you're part of is increasingly fractured about what these stories should mean.

stories.headline: The Rolling Stones: Foreign Tongues Album Review - Pitchfork
stories.summary: Read Stuart Berman’s review of the album.
stories.why: Without knowing if this is a comeback or legacy cash grab, this headline tells you nothing; check the actual review to understand if the Stones still matter or if they're nostalgia now.

stories.headline: Security breach causes chaos, delays start of final Jay-Z concert at Yankee Stadium - ABC7 New York
stories.summary: A police source tells Eyewitness News a large group broke through security, prompting a full lockdown of the stadium.
stories.why: Jay-Z's final Yankee Stadium show collapsing under its own hype is a cultural inflection point—it's the moment the biggest rapper's victory lap becomes a logistical embarrassment.

stories.headline: Stephen Chow’s ‘Kung Fu Soccer’ Kicks Off With Mighty $74 Million Two-Day Debut at China Box Office - The Hollywood Reporter
stories.summary: The 'Shaolin Soccer' spinoff, Stephen Chow's first film as director in seven years, is projected to earn more than $350 million in its home market, with plans for a U.S. release still in the works.
stories.why: A $74M opening for a kung-fu comedy spinoff in China signals that global box office power has fundamentally shifted East, and American studios now chase what works there, not the reverse.

stories.headline: Josh Grisetti Dead: Broadway Actor Appeared On 'The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel' - Deadline
stories.summary: Josh Grisetti, the Broadway actor best known for Something Rotten! and It Shoulda Been You died Friday by suicide.
stories.why: A recognizable Broadway talent's suicide amid post-*Maisel* career uncertainty reflects the brutal reality of that industry—visible success doesn't equal financial security or mental stability.

stories.headline: The real Daeron Targaryen speaks: House of the Dragon star unpacks the ‘inherent conflict of power’ with Ormund - Entertainment Weekly
stories.summary: ‘House of the Dragon’ star behind Daeron Targaryen (the real one) speaks. The actor talks the ‘uncontrollable, chaotic’ Ormund and the ‘inherent conflict of power.’
stories.why: This is fan-service interview content; skip unless you're actively invested in *House of the Dragon* lore.
